preview_text:
The model favors Senegal at 60%, with Iraq given a 13% chance of victory. The gap reflects a substantial difference in estimated strength, though the underdog retains meaningful probability of a result.

The draw carries 27% probability in the model's forecast, the largest single outcome in the trio. This suggests the match could prove competitive enough to deadlock, rather than a one-sided affair. The win probabilities are separated by roughly 48 percentage points, indicating clear stratification without an overwhelmingly dominant favorite.

The sportsbook consensus sits notably higher on Senegal, at 67%, a gap of 6 percentage points above the model. Polymarket prices align more closely with books, placing Senegal at 69%. Both market sources compress the draw probability to around 21%, well below the model's 27%. The divergence suggests books assign less probability to a stalemate outcome. The dashboard makes no claim about which estimate is correct; both remain plausible given forecast uncertainty.
